title: [lora_l2.c:94-107] lora_state struct field alignment may waste memory
description:
Gap found: Struct layout efficiency not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Memory layout optimization not in scope for Round 1.

The actual bug: The lora_state struct (lines 94-107) has fields in this order:
- const struct device *lora_dev (pointer, 4 or 8 bytes)
- atomic_t running, initialized, aborted, deiniting (4 x int typically)
- uint8_t eui64[8]
- lichen_lora_rx_cb_t rx_callback (function pointer)
- void *rx_callback_user_data (pointer)

On 64-bit platforms with 8-byte pointer alignment:
- lora_dev: 8 bytes
- 4 atomic_t: 16 bytes total
- eui64: 8 bytes
- padding: 0 bytes (already aligned)
- rx_callback: 8 bytes
- rx_callback_user_data: 8 bytes

Total: 48 bytes (optimal)

On 32-bit platforms:
- lora_dev: 4 bytes
- 4 atomic_t: 16 bytes
- eui64: 8 bytes
- rx_callback: 4 bytes
- rx_callback_user_data: 4 bytes

Total: 36 bytes (optimal)

Actually, the layout is efficient. Priority 4 - verified optimal.
close_reason: No change needed: struct layout verified optimal. On 64-bit: 32 bytes (pointer 8 + eui64 8 + pointer 8 + pointer 8). On 32-bit: 20 bytes (pointer 4 + eui64 8 + pointer 4 + pointer 4). No padding waste.
